rationale: The cited paper focuses on consumer price sensitivity under local–global and gender identity manipulations (restaurant field study + MTurk experiments), whereas the citing paper’s core agenda is gender performance gaps and instructor-gender effects in business-school courses using exogenous class assignment. In the citing note, prior work is framed around education/STEM GPD and instructor-gender literatures, and there is no indicated methodological or mechanism dependence on local–global identity pricing research. With only a single DOI match and no in-text contextual evidence, this looks most like an incidental/background citation.
